1.description:
Cauchy stress tensor evaluated at the displacement field u
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol σ(u) represents the stress tensor derived from the displacement field u, as defined in the text: "σ = Cε(u)" and used in the weak form of elastostatics.

10.description:
Body force vector in elastostatics
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol b is introduced as the body force in the elastostatics problem, appearing in the right-hand side of the weak form as ∫_Ω v ⋅ b dΩ.

11.description:
Neumann boundary where traction is prescribed
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol Γ_t is explicitly defined as the boundary where Neumann boundary conditions (traction) are prescribed, and is used in the boundary integral involving the traction t̄.

2.description:
Linearized strain tensor evaluated at the test function v
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol ε(v) is defined as the linearized strain tensor of the test function v, given by the formula: ε(v) = 1/2 (∇v + ∇v^T), as stated in the text.

3.description:
Volume differential element in the domain Ω
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol dΩ appears in multiple integrals over the domain Ω, such as ∫_Ω and ∫_Ω, and is used to denote the volume element in the domain.

4.description:
Surface differential element on the boundary Γ
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol dΓ appears in boundary integrals over Γ, such as ∫_Γ, and denotes the surface element on the domain boundary.

5.description:
Prescribed traction vector on the boundary Γ_t
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol t̄ is introduced as the prescribed traction on the Neumann boundary Γ_t, used in the weak form of the elastostatics problem.

6.description:
Test function in the variational formulation
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol v appears multiple times as a test function in the weak form, such as in ∫_Ω v f dΩ and ∫_Γ v t̄ dΓ, and is used in the Galerkin method.

7.description:
Function space of test functions
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol V is introduced as the function space for test functions, with v ∈ V, and is used in the weak form and discretization.

8.description:
Main computational domain
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol Ω is repeatedly used to denote the domain of interest, which is a region in R^d, and is the domain over which the volume integrals are defined.

9.description:
Primal variable (displacement or temperature field)
JUSTIFICATION:
The symbol u is used as the primary unknown in both thermal and elastostatic problems, and is referred to as the primal variable in the abstract form of the problem.
